speaker:
FIRST
               
              PETITIONER
text: My masters, let’s stand close. My

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PETITIONER
text: Lord Protector will come this way by and by, and

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PETITIONER
text: then we may deliver our supplications in the quill.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              PETITIONER
text: Marry, the Lord protect him, for

speaker:
SECOND
               
              PETITIONER
text: he’s a good man! Jesu bless him!

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PETITIONER
text: Here he comes, methinks, and the

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PETITIONER
text: Queen with him. I’ll be the first, sure.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              PETITIONER
text: Come back, fool! This is the Duke

speaker:
SECOND
               
              PETITIONER
text: of Suffolk, and not my Lord Protector.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: How now, fellow? Wouldst anything with

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: me?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PETITIONER
text: I pray, my lord, pardon me. I took

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PETITIONER
text: you for my Lord Protector.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text:
To
                 
                my
                
                Lord
                 
                Protector
                .
               Are your supplications to his Lordship?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Let me see them.—What is thine?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PETITIONER
text: Mine is, an ’t please your Grace,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PETITIONER
text: against John Goodman, my Lord Cardinal’s man,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PETITIONER
text: for keeping my house, and lands, and wife and all,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              PETITIONER
text: from me.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thy wife too? That’s some wrong indeed.—

speaker: SUFFOLK
text:
What’s yours?
                 
                Taking
                 
                a
                 
                petition
                .
               What’s here?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text:
(
                Reads
                .
                )
               
                Against
                 
                the
                 
                Duke
                 
                of
                 
                Suffolk
                 
                for
                 
                enclosing
                
                the
                 
                commons
                 
                of
                 
                Melford
                .
               How now, sir knave?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              PETITIONER
text: Alas, sir, I am but a poor petitioner

speaker:
SECOND
               
              PETITIONER
text: of our whole township.

speaker: PETER
text: Against my master,

speaker: PETER
text: Thomas Horner, for saying that the Duke of York

speaker: PETER
text: was rightful heir to the crown.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What sayst thou? Did the Duke of

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: York say he was rightful heir to the crown?

speaker: PETER
text: That my master was? No, forsooth. My master

speaker: PETER
text: said that he was and that the King was an

speaker: PETER
text: usurper.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Who is there?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Take this fellow in, and send for his master with a

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: pursuivant presently.—We’ll hear more of your

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: matter before the King.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And as for you that love to be protected

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Under the wings of our Protector’s grace,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Begin your suits anew, and sue to him.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Away, base cullions.—Suffolk, let them go.

speaker: ALL
text: Come, let’s be gone.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: My lord of Suffolk, say, is this the guise,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Is this the fashions in the court of England?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Is this the government of Britain’s isle

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And this the royalty of Albion’s king?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What, shall King Henry be a pupil still

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Under the surly Gloucester’s governance?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Am I a queen in title and in style,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And must be made a subject to a duke?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I tell thee, Pole, when in the city Tours

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thou rann’st atilt in honor of my love

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And stol’st away the ladies’ hearts of France,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I thought King Henry had resembled thee

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: In courage, courtship, and proportion.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But all his mind is bent to holiness,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To number Ave Marys on his beads;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: His champions are the prophets and apostles,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: His weapons holy saws of sacred writ,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: His study is his tiltyard, and his loves

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Are brazen images of canonized saints.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I would the College of the Cardinals

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Would choose him pope and carry him to Rome

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And set the triple crown upon his head!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That were a state fit for his holiness.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Madam, be patient. As I was cause

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Your Highness came to England, so will I

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: In England work your Grace’s full content.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Besides the haughty Protector, have we Beaufort

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The imperious churchman, Somerset, Buckingham,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And grumbling York; and not the least of these

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But can do more in England than the King.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And he of these that can do most of all

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Cannot do more in England than the Nevilles;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Salisbury and Warwick are no simple peers.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Not all these lords do vex me half so much

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: As that proud dame, the Lord Protector’s wife.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: She sweeps it through the court with troops of ladies,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: More like an empress than Duke Humphrey’s wife.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Strangers in court do take her for the Queen.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: She bears a duke’s revenues on her back,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And in her heart she scorns our poverty.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Shall I not live to be avenged on her?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Contemptuous baseborn callet as she is,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: She vaunted ’mongst her minions t’ other day

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The very train of her worst wearing gown

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Was better worth than all my father’s lands

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Till Suffolk gave two dukedoms for his daughter.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Madam, myself have limed a bush for her

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And placed a choir of such enticing birds

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That she will light to listen to the lays

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And never mount to trouble you again.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: So let her rest. And, madam, list to me,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: For I am bold to counsel you in this:

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Although we fancy not the Cardinal,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Yet must we join with him and with the lords

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Till we have brought Duke Humphrey in disgrace.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As for the Duke of York, this late complaint

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Will make but little for his benefit.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: So, one by one, we’ll weed them all at last,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And you yourself shall steer the happy helm.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For my part, noble lords, I care not which;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Or Somerset or York, all’s one to me.

speaker: YORK
text: If York have ill demeaned himself in France,

speaker: YORK
text: Then let him be denied the regentship.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: If Somerset be unworthy of the place,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Let York be regent; I will yield to him.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Whether your Grace be worthy, yea or no,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Dispute not that. York is the worthier.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Ambitious Warwick, let thy betters speak.

speaker: WARWICK
text: The Cardinal’s not my better in the field.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: All in this presence are thy betters, Warwick.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Warwick may live to be the best of all.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Peace, son.—And show some reason, Buckingham,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Why Somerset should be preferred in this.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Because the King, forsooth, will have it so.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Madam, the King is old enough himself

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To give his censure. These are no women’s matters.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: If he be old enough, what needs your Grace

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To be Protector of his Excellence?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Madam, I am Protector of the realm,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And at his pleasure will resign my place.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Resign it, then, and leave thine insolence.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Since thou wert king—as who is king but thou?—

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The commonwealth hath daily run to wrack,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The Dauphin hath prevailed beyond the seas,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And all the peers and nobles of the realm

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Have been as bondmen to thy sovereignty.

speaker: CARDINAL
text: The Commons hast thou racked; the clergy’s bags

speaker: CARDINAL
text: Are lank and lean with thy extortions.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Thy sumptuous buildings and thy wife’s attire

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Have cost a mass of public treasury.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Thy cruelty in execution

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Upon offenders hath exceeded law

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And left thee to the mercy of the law.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thy sale of offices and towns in France,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: If they were known, as the suspect is great,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Would make thee quickly hop without thy head.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text:
To
                 
                Duchess
                .
                 
              Give me my fan. What, minion, can you not?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I cry you mercy, madam. Was it you?

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Was ’t I? Yea, I it was, proud Frenchwoman.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Could I come near your beauty with my nails,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: I’d set my ten commandments in your face.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Sweet aunt, be quiet. ’Twas against her will.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Against her will, good king? Look to ’t in time.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: She’ll hamper thee and dandle thee like a baby.

speaker: DUCHESS
text: Though in this place most master wear no breeches,

speaker: DUCHESS
text: She shall not strike Dame Eleanor unrevenged.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Lord Cardinal, I will follow Eleanor

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And listen after Humphrey how he proceeds.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: She’s tickled now; her fume needs no spurs;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: She’ll gallop far enough to her destruction.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Now, lords, my choler being overblown

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: With walking once about the quadrangle,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I come to talk of commonwealth affairs.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: As for your spiteful false objections,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Prove them, and I lie open to the law;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: But God in mercy so deal with my soul

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: As I in duty love my king and country!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: But, to the matter that we have in hand:

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I say, my sovereign, York is meetest man

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To be your regent in the realm of France.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Before we make election, give me leave

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To show some reason, of no little force,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That York is most unmeet of any man.

speaker: YORK
text: I’ll tell thee, Suffolk, why I am unmeet:

speaker: YORK
text: First, for I cannot flatter thee in pride;

speaker: YORK
text: Next, if I be appointed for the place,

speaker: YORK
text: My lord of Somerset will keep me here

speaker: YORK
text: Without discharge, money, or furniture

speaker: YORK
text: Till France be won into the Dauphin’s hands.

speaker: YORK
text: Last time I danced attendance on his will

speaker: YORK
text: Till Paris was besieged, famished, and lost.

speaker: WARWICK
text: That can I witness, and a fouler fact

speaker: WARWICK
text: Did never traitor in the land commit.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Peace, headstrong Warwick!

speaker: WARWICK
text: Image of pride, why should I hold my peace?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Because here is a man accused of treason.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Pray God the Duke of York excuse himself!

speaker: YORK
text: Doth anyone accuse York for a traitor?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What mean’st thou, Suffolk? Tell me, what are these?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Please it your Majesty, this is the man

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That doth accuse his master of high treason.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: His words were these: that Richard, Duke of York,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Was rightful heir unto the English crown,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And that your Majesty was an usurper.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Say, man, were these thy words?

speaker: HORNER
text: An ’t shall please your Majesty, I never said

speaker: HORNER
text: nor thought any such matter. God is my witness, I

speaker: HORNER
text: am falsely accused by the villain.

speaker: PETER
text: By these ten bones, my lords, he did speak

speaker: PETER
text: them to me in the garret one night as we were

speaker: PETER
text: scouring my lord of York’s armor.

speaker: YORK
text: Base dunghill villain and mechanical,

speaker: YORK
text: I’ll have thy head for this thy traitor’s speech!—

speaker: YORK
text: I do beseech your royal Majesty,

speaker: YORK
text: Let him have all the rigor of the law.

speaker: HORNER
text: Alas, my lord, hang me if ever I spake the

speaker: HORNER
text: words. My accuser is my prentice; and when I did

speaker: HORNER
text: correct him for his fault the other day, he did vow

speaker: HORNER
text: upon his knees he would be even with me. I have

speaker: HORNER
text: good witness of this. Therefore I beseech your

speaker: HORNER
text: Majesty, do not cast away an honest man for a

speaker: HORNER
text: villain’s accusation!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Uncle, what shall we say to this in law?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: This doom, my lord, if I may judge:

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Let Somerset be regent o’er the French,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Because in York this breeds suspicion;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And let these have a day appointed them

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: For single combat in convenient place,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: For he hath witness of his servant’s malice.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: This is the law, and this Duke Humphrey’s doom.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: I humbly thank your royal Majesty.

speaker: HORNER
text: And I accept the combat willingly.

speaker: PETER
text: Alas, my lord, I cannot fight; for God’s sake pity

speaker: PETER
text: my case! The spite of man prevaileth against me. O

speaker: PETER
text: Lord, have mercy upon me! I shall never be able to

speaker: PETER
text: fight a blow. O Lord, my heart!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Sirrah, or you must fight or else be hanged.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Away with them to prison; and the day of

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: combat shall be the last of the next month.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Come, Somerset, we’ll see thee sent away.
